name: Personal Finance Owner
role: Sole owner and decision-maker for household finances
goals: When reviewing or planning personal spending, I want one trustworthy view across all owned accounts, evidence sources, spend pools, and categories so I can delegate maintenance safely and know why each transaction is present.
pain_points: Daily notifications and later statements can describe the same economic event; transactions are fragmented across three accounts at two banks; account, cardholder, payment instrument, pool, and category can be conflated; manual consolidation has weak provenance and uncertain duplicate handling.
workaround: Inspect separate notifications and statements, manually decide whether rows describe the same transaction, consolidate selected transactions, infer account or cardholder context, remove transfers or duplicates, assign categories and spending pools, and recalculate totals.
success: The owner can delegate daily capture and monthly reconciliation, review every ambiguous or missing match, and retrieve exact pool/category totals whose evidence and decision history are inspectable.
